context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements Medusa v2 event subscribers and custom workflows, including proper TypeScript imports, using the DI container to resolve services, re-fetching entity data inside the subscriber, and adding compensation functions to workflow steps that have side effects.

checklist.name: SubscriberArgs/SubscriberConfig import
checklist.description: Subscriber file imports SubscriberArgs and/or SubscriberConfig from '@medusajs/framework' (not from a sub-path like /utils)

checklist.name: Subscriber config export
checklist.description: Subscriber file exports a `config` object of type SubscriberConfig containing an `event` field with the event name string

checklist.name: container.resolve() in subscriber
checklist.description: All services used inside the subscriber are obtained via container.resolve() rather than direct imports

checklist.name: Entity re-fetched in subscriber
checklist.description: The subscriber retrieves the full entity data (e.g. order) from a service using the ID from the event payload, rather than relying solely on the event payload for entity fields

checklist.name: Subscriber for side effects only
checklist.description: The subscriber delegates substantive processing to a workflow or service call rather than containing inline core business logic

checklist.name: createWorkflow / createStep imports
checklist.description: Workflow file imports createWorkflow, createStep, and StepResponse from '@medusajs/framework/workflows-sdk'

checklist.name: StepResponse returned from step
checklist.description: Each createStep handler returns a new StepResponse(...) rather than returning a plain object or value

checklist.name: Compensation function on side-effect step
checklist.description: At least one step that writes data or calls an external API includes a compensation function (third argument to createStep) for rollback

checklist.name: Compensation data passed to StepResponse
checklist.description: The StepResponse for a step with a compensation function passes rollback data as the second argument (e.g. new StepResponse(result, { id: result.id }))

checklist.name: container.resolve() in workflow step
checklist.description: Services accessed inside workflow steps are resolved from the container (second argument to the step handler), not imported directly

checklist.name: Correct file placement
checklist.description: Subscriber file is placed under src/subscribers/ and workflow file under src/workflows/

checklist.name: Logger resolved from container
checklist.description: Logging uses a logger resolved from the container (container.resolve('logger')) rather than console.log
